title: Workload Distribution Limitations in Monday.com
excerpt:
Description
It’s frustrating that we don’t have better control over how items are distributed in the workload view, this feels like a basic expectation of a resource management tool, and Monday.com really needs to addres…

posts.author: deepsangar (Deep Sangar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

It’s frustrating that we don’t have better control over how items are distributed in the workload view, this feels like a basic expectation of a resource management tool, and Monday.com really needs to address this.
For example, with a 7.5-hour working day, if one task requires 6.5 hours and another 4 hours, I should be able to prioritise the first task (e.g. 6.5 hours on day 1, with 1 hour of the second task), with the remaining 3 hours rolling into day 2.
Instead, hours are spread evenly, which doesn’t reflect real workflows and creates unbalanced days, making resource planning unnecessarily difficult.
I’ve raised this with Monday.com and was told, “this is not a technical issue”, which is quite baffling given how fundamental this is.

What are you trying to achieve

I’m trying to ensure workloads are realistic and balanced by being able to prioritise tasks and more importantly control how hours are allocated across days.

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @deepsangar I’m not sure I have a direct solution to your issue but if you are working on resource management it might be worth taking a look at SuperPlanner, a marketplace app that takes holidays and vacations into account and gives you a nicer view of assignments directly from the board. Check it out: SuperPlanner - monday.com Marketplace and learn more: https://spot-nik.com/product/superplanner/
